rect.data-narration: Iteration refers to the process of repeating a set of instructions multiple times. In parallel computing, iterations are used to execute tasks concurrently, improving the overall performance of a program.

path.data-narration: Data races occur when multiple threads or processes access shared data simultaneously, causing data corruption and incorrect results.
Cache thrashing occurs when multiple threads or processes access different parts of the cache, reducing performance.

rect.data-narration: The number of iterations determines the amount of work to be done. Too many iterations can lead to underutilization of hardware and incorrect results.

path.data-narration: Too many iterations can lead to underutilization of hardware, reducing performance.
Incorrect results can occur if the number of iterations is too low, as the program may not have executed enough iterations to converge to the correct solution.

rect.data-narration: Use a fixed number of iterations instead of a dynamic number. This ensures that the program executes the correct number of iterations.

path.data-narration: Consider the problem size when specifying the number of iterations. The number of iterations should be proportional to the problem size.
